key_insight: ATLAS learns a diffusion-based sampler that directly generates Boltzmann-distributed amorphous structures from an energy function, enabling efficient sampling, estimation, and design in rugged glassy landscapes.
engineering_relevance: This could dramatically reduce the cost of simulating and optimizing metallic glasses and other amorphous materials, accelerating inverse design of strength, ductility, and thermodynamic properties.
method: Equivariant graph neural network parameterizes a reversible diffusion sampler.
Uses time reversal to estimate thermodynamic quantities and steer toward target observables.
Supports composition-amortized pretraining and coupling to expensive interatomic potentials or LLM-guided search.
evidence_signal: On 2D Kob-Andersen glasses, ATLAS matched parallel-tempering MCMC distributions and achieved below 0.2% free-energy error in the low-temperature regime with over 500-fold fewer energy evaluations; it also reproduced experimental short-range-order trends in Cu-Zr and Cr-Co-Ni systems.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the target energy model and may be sensitive when transferring to new chemistries, system sizes, or observables outside the training distribution.
paper_title: ATLAS: A Foundation Neural Sampler for Amorphous Materials
